findings.title: Unconditional test for Go 1.22+ JSON encoding
findings.rationale: The diff removed the Go version check that previously skipped the test for \b/\f characters in older Go versions. The test now runs unconditionally, causing failures in Go versions <1.22 where json.Marshal("\b\f") produces "\b\f" instead of "\\b\\f". The test expects correct JSON encoding which only exists in Go 1.22+.
findings.suggestion: Reintroduce the Go version check to conditionally run the test only in Go 1.22+

evidence.ref: TestJSONString uses Go standard library json.Marshal which changed behavior in Go 1.22
